hero_subtitle: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific MiCA rules, provider outputs, and case workflows to help CASPs maintain traceable reporting and audit-ready evidence across multiple regulatory obligations.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Reporting workflows become slow and fragmented
problem_description: Compliance teams managing MiCA obligations struggle with disconnected data sources and manual coordination that delay reporting processes. This fragmentation weakens audit readiness and obscures clear traceability across regulatory case evidence and internal compliance actions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects provider data, regulatory updates, case management and evidence preservation into a unified workflow that streamlines MiCA reporting while improving audit readiness and internal traceability for CASP compliance teams.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to MiCA reporting
workflow_description: SFTY monitors jurisdiction-specific regulatory changes, maps updates to internal rules, structures cases and preserves evidence for MiCA compliance reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented outputs. One MiCA evidence layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific provider results into structured cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence to support MiCA compliance reporting workflows for CASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports CASPs managing jurisdiction-specific MiCA reporting with structured workflows, audit-ready evidence preservation and secure enterprise deployment for institutional trust and due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MiCA compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a connected compliance layer that connects provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports tailored to MiCA regulatory requirements for CASPs and digital asset firms.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure MiCA reporting workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes MiCA reporting by linking alerts, transactions, and onboarding data into case workflows, supporting regulatory rule updates and preparing audit-ready evidence packages for MiCA reports.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready MiCA evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed decision logs, supporting evidence and case histories to maintain clear audit trails aligned with MiCA compliance and supervisory review needs.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing MiCA providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers to aggregate their outputs into one compliant MiCA workflow without disrupting current setups.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace MiCA compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above providers and structures their outputs; it does not replace MiCA compliance tools but enhances operational control and audit readiness.
final_title: Structure MiCA reporting workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY organizes jurisdiction-specific rules, provider outputs and evidence into controlled MiCA compliance cases and reports.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
